panels.title: Prediction latency (p50/p95/p99) - stable vs canary
panels.description: Sourced from Prometheus, scraped from model-server's /metrics (Project 4's Prometheus instance, reused). If the canary is meaningfully slower than stable, that alone can be a reason not to promote it, independent of accuracy.

panels.title: Prediction request rate - stable vs canary (should track the configured weight)
panels.description: 80/20 configured in helm/model-server/values.yaml should show up here as roughly an 80/20 request-count split - this panel is the actual proof the weighted Ingress routing is working, not just configured.

panels.title: Drift score (PSI) by feature
panels.description: Published by monitoring/drift_detector.py every run. PSI > 0.25 (the red threshold line) is what triggers the automated retraining Step Functions workflow.

panels.title: Model accuracy over time (by model type, each evaluation run)
panels.description: Published by evaluation/evaluate.py every pipeline run - one point per model type per evaluation, whether or not that run resulted in a new registered model. Watch for a declining trend across retrains, not just the absolute value.

panels.title: Fraud-flagged rate (data quality / sanity proxy)
panels.description: A crude but effective data-quality signal: if this suddenly jumps to near-0% or near-100%, it's far more likely a broken upstream feature pipeline than an actual real-world fraud pattern change - worth a sanity check before trusting a drift alert.
